demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a live-in relative who acts as carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My stomach is so tight I can't catch my breath, and I feel like I'm in a fog. (His live-in relative is on the call and says he's been getting more and more swollen and muddled.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His belly has been swelling up all week and got much worse in the last day — now he can't breathe properly and he's confused, and it's after hours so we couldn't reach the usual doctor.

symptom_narrative.onset: belly swelling has crept up over the past week, then got a lot worse over the last 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: about a week, sharply worse in the last day
symptom_narrative.character: belly feels hugely swollen, tight and hard; breathing feels tight and he can't get a full breath; his relative says he's foggy, drowsy and muddled about what day it is
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: he says he can't catch his breath and feels like he's in a fog; he's had to be propped up on pillows to breathe
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mostly the belly and the chest/breathing; a general all-over weakness
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse, worst when lying flat
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting propped up on several pillows helps his breathing a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down flat makes the breathlessness worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: long-standing yellow tinge to his eyes and skin; feeling generally weak and washed out; carer noticed he's been unusually sleepy and unsteady with a couple of recent falls
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has known long-standing liver trouble and has had a swollen belly needing attention before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound today, propped up on pillows; his relative can't safely get him up to move around

past_medical_history.how_managed: usually on water tablets and a syrup laxative, followed up by his own doctor

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing yellowing of the eyes and skin

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with a relative who is his carer; the carer says he has missed several doses of his water tablet and hasn't taken his laxative syrup for about the last 4 days; a couple of recent falls at home with bruising on the forearms; carer feels unable to safely lift or mobilise him

objective_data_offered.value: belly looks hugely swollen, tight and shiny; he is sitting propped upright and breathing fast and with effort
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer's video view over chat, not a clinical examination

objective_data_offered.value: the whites of his eyes and his skin look yellow
he is drowsy, muddled and disoriented to the day, but still wakes/rouses when spoken to
he can't breathe comfortably lying down and has to stay propped up on several pillows
bruising on both forearms from a couple of recent falls at home
